tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: The fail-to-pass tests (test_filter_file_mechanism_removed, test_filter_option_removed, test_debug_cli_option, test_interactive_watch_mode, test_watch_runner_signature_updated, test_claudemd_documents_cli) all exclusively use string matching on file contents (includes(), regex tests). None of them import modules, call functions, or execute the CLI. The pass-to-pass tests do run builds/compiles but those test existing behavior, not new behavior.

test_not_tautological.reason: A stub/empty implementation wouldn't pass because the tests check both for removal of old code (e.g., FILTER_FILENAME, readTestFilter, subscribeFilterFile must not be present) AND addition of new code (e.g., .boolean('debug'), inputMode, key.name === 'p' must be present), plus TypeScript must compile. You can't satisfy both by doing nothing.

anti_cheating_measures.reason: The solution is available in /workspace/react via git fetch of the merge commit (solve.sh shows 'git fetch origin 03613cd68c9e1d7ec5cb1eceb91f27f1935a7b8b'). An agent could run the same git commands to obtain the exact gold patch. The Dockerfile doesn't remove .git or restrict network access.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All major behavioral requirements from the instruction are tested: removal of filter file mechanism, addition of --debug/-d CLI option, interactive watch mode with p/d/a keys, updated makeWatchRunner signature, and CLAUDE.md creation with required content.

This is excessive localization that removes the need for the agent to reason about implementation.
